one_time_description: Impairment loss on youth centre tangible capital asset written down from $12,100,000 to $2,500,000 due to damages from COVID-19 construction suspension
economic_notes: Nation impacted by COVID-19 travel restrictions, stoppages on major capital projects, increased PPE expenditures, decreased ability to collect arrears. Attawapiskat Enterprises includes catering, security, and resource businesses. Impact Benefit Agreement with De Beers Canada. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership gaming revenue distribution. Winter road construction via Kimesskanemenow Limited Partnership. Nation operating under ISC Management Action Plan.
notes: Audit is qualified due to absence of budget comparison figures (not a material misstatement qualification). $9.6M impairment loss on youth centre building written down due to COVID-19 construction suspension damages. Nation operating under ISC Management Action Plan. Two GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis: Attawapiskat Power Corporation (Dec 31 year-end) and Attawapiskat Enterprises. Five entities fully consolidated. Tangible capital assets net book value not extractable from text provided (Schedule 1 referenced but not fully reproduced). CMHC reserve fund deficiency of $470,008. Repayable to funding agencies totalling $3,327,241 (ISC, Health Canada, Ministry of Health). ISC funding calculated as Fixed + Grant + Flexible + Set + Prior year adjustments = $29,853,928. Total government transfers include ISC + FNIHB + CMHC + Nishnawbe Aski + Province of Ontario.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
